The Journey

Building Bridges Between Tech and Soil.

I believe that even the most advanced AI is meaningless if it doesn't solve a real human problem on a real farm.

If you had told me a decade ago, while I was finishing my Ph.D. in Law, that I would spend my days measuring leaves and discussing seedling viability in forests, I wouldn't have believed you.

My transition from legal scholarship to founding Petiole wasn't just a career change; it was a response to a "brutal truth." I saw a gap where technology was too complex for the people who actually needed it. My legal training taught me how systems are built, but my life as a founder taught me how they are broken—and fixed.

The "Before Sunrise" Mindset

Moving from a small town in Ukraine to Heathrow Airport with a UK Global Talent Visa wasn't about luck. It was about waking up before the sun to bridge the distance between where I was and where I needed to be. I bring that same relentless energy to every policy briefing and every line of computer vision code we develop.

Today, my work sits at the intersection of three worlds: the precision of AI, the rigor of policy, and the reality of the field. Whether I’m advising the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) or working with fellow innovators at the Oxford Farming Conference, my goal is the same: to make technology feel human and innovation feel inevitable.

What I'm doing now

  • OFC 2026: Learning and leading as an Inspire Fellow.
  • Innovate UK: Championing women in tech through the Community Forum.
  • Petiole: Solving the "un-solvable" in plant phenotyping.

The Pillars

Ph.D. • Law & Logic

CEO • Grit & Vision

Advisor • Global Impact

Dr. Maryna Kuzmenko

Architecting the future of agrifood through AI, Policy, and Human-First Innovation.
